title: Implement approved Pixel trace and observation tooling
summary: Collect sanitized black-box and stock-system observations needed for native contract tests without copying protected implementation or personal data.

title: Reach lawful reproducible Agent OS early diagnostics on Pixel 9
summary: Determine whether the Pixel boot chain can load Agent OS far enough to produce independent early diagnostics without undocumented portable dependencies.

title: Evaluate native Pixel storage, USB, display, and touch paths
summary: Prove or bound the route to persistent storage, USB debug/recovery, first frame and touch without leaking vendor interfaces into portable services.

title: Evaluate native Pixel audio and basic device-service paths
summary: Bound speaker/microphone, buttons/haptics, sensors and basic power-state integrations without asserting modem/camera readiness.

title: Evaluate Pixel GPU, display acceleration, power, and thermal feasibility
summary: Determine whether a bounded lawful native GPU/display acceleration and safe power/thermal route exists at acceptable effort.

title: Evaluate native Pixel camera feasibility and quality gap
summary: Map sensors, CSI, ISP, lens/OIS, metadata, calibration, firmware, tuning, power and legal rights to determine a native or controlled-adapter route.

title: Audit Pixel track source, license, security, and publication readiness
summary: Review every Pixel artifact, specification, trace, adapter, code contribution, dependency and public claim before gate decision or release.

title: Issue Pixel 9 continue, limit, or stop gate decision
summary: Decide whether native Pixel work continues, remains a quality/sidecar target, or stops based on evidence, legal rights, effort, opportunity cost and portable architecture impact.
